seoTitle: ZLG Power Solutions | Industrial Power Design | LiTong
seoDescription: Complete power solutions using ZLG Power DC-DC converters, AC-DC supplies, and POL modules for industrial automation, telecommunications, and medical equipment.

faqs.question: What types of power solutions does ZLG Power offer for industrial applications?
faqs.answer: ZLG Power provides comprehensive power solutions including isolated DC-DC converter systems for industrial control, AC-DC power supplies for equipment, POL converters for processor power, and custom power solutions for specialized applications. These solutions leverage ZLG Power's high-reliability modules to deliver efficient, compact power conversion for industrial automation, telecommunications, medical equipment, and transportation systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore our solution categories to find the right ZLG Power solution for your application, or contact our FAE team for personalized recommendations.

faqs.question: How can I get technical support for implementing ZLG Power modules in my design?
faqs.answer: LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for ZLG Power module implementation. Our FAE team offers schematic review, PCB layout recommendations, thermal design consultation, and troubleshooting assistance. Contact our technical support team or submit a support ticket through our website for assistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team early in your design phase for optimal implementation guidance.

faqs.question: What are the key advantages of using ZLG Power modules in distributed power systems?
faqs.answer: ZLG Power modules offer significant advantages for distributed power systems: High isolation voltages (1500V-3000V) enable safe power distribution; Wide input ranges accommodate varying bus voltages; Compact packages save board space; High efficiency reduces heat generation; Comprehensive protection features ensure system reliability; Standard pinouts simplify design and sourcing.
faqs.decisionGuide: For distributed power systems, select modules with appropriate isolation and input voltage ranges for your architecture.

faqs.question: Does ZLG Power provide reference designs for their power modules?
faqs.answer: Yes, ZLG Power provides comprehensive reference designs including complete schematics, PCB layout guidelines, BOM lists, and thermal management recommendations. These reference designs are available for industrial control systems, telecommunications equipment, medical devices, and LED lighting applications. Contact LiTong Electronics to request specific reference designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use reference designs as starting points for your application, modifying as needed for your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What thermal management solutions are recommended for high-power ZLG Power modules?
faqs.answer: High-power ZLG Power modules require proper thermal management: Natural convection for modules under 10W; Heatsinks with thermal resistance appropriate for the application; Forced air cooling for high-power or high-density applications; Thermal interface materials with low thermal resistance; Thermal simulation to verify temperatures remain within limits under all operating conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal modeling and heatsink selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right input capacitor for ZLG Power DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Input capacitor selection depends on the module type and application: For 1W-3W modules: 0.1-1uF ceramic capacitor close to input pins; For 5W-10W modules: 1-10uF ceramic plus optional electrolytic for high ripple current; For AC-DC modules: Follow datasheet EMI filter recommendations; Place capacitors as close as possible to input pins; Use low-ESR ceramic capacitors (X7R or X5R dielectric) for best performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow datasheet recommendations and contact FAE for specific filtering requirements.

faqs.question: What is the typical lead time for ZLG Power products?
faqs.answer: Standard lead time for ZLG Power products is 4-6 weeks for production quantities. For high-volume orders, long-term supply agreements can be established to secure capacity. LiTong Electronics maintains inventory of popular models for immediate delivery. Contact our sales team for current availability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan procurement with standard lead times, or contact us for stock availability and expedited delivery.

faqs.question: Are ZLG Power modules qualified for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, ZLG Power offers automotive-qualified modules with AEC-Q100 certification and IATF 16949 manufacturing. These modules feature extended temperature ranges, enhanced reliability screening, and meet automotive quality requirements. For specific automotive qualification needs, contact LiTong Electronics.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, specify AEC-Q qualified modules and request qualification documentation.

solutions.title: Industrial Control System Power Solution
solutions.description: Complete power solution using ZLG Power isolated DC-DC converters and AC-DC supplies for industrial automation applications.
solutions.longDescription: This comprehensive power solution leverages ZLG Power's isolated DC-DC converters and AC-DC power supplies to deliver reliable, efficient power for industrial control systems. The solution supports distributed power architectures with isolation for safety and noise immunity, making it suitable for PLCs, motor drives, sensors, and industrial I/O systems.

faqs.question: What power architecture does this solution use?
faqs.answer: The solution uses a distributed power architecture with AC-DC front-end converting mains power to 24V DC bus, followed by isolated DC-DC converters providing regulated voltages for control circuits, sensors, and communication interfaces. This architecture provides isolation, flexibility, and easy expansion.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact FAE for architecture recommendations based on your system power requirements.

faqs.question: What isolation levels are provided?
faqs.answer: The solution provides 3000V isolation between AC mains and 24V bus, and 1500V-3000V isolation between 24V bus and individual control circuits. This meets requirements for industrial safety standards and provides excellent noise immunity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify isolation requirements for your specific application and safety standards.

faqs.question: How does the solution handle voltage transients?
faqs.answer: The solution includes comprehensive transient protection: Input surge protection on AC side; TVS diodes on DC bus; Isolation barriers prevent transients from propagating; Each DC-DC module has built-in protection. This ensures reliable operation in harsh industrial environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: For severe transient environments, additional external protection may be recommended.

faqs.question: What is the typical efficiency of this solution?
faqs.answer: Overall system efficiency is typically 75-80%: AC-DC front-end: 85-88% efficiency; DC-DC converters: 80-85% efficiency; Combined efficiency accounts for distribution losses. High efficiency reduces heat generation and operating costs.
faqs.decisionGuide: For energy-critical applications, select high-efficiency modules and optimize loading.

faqs.question: Can this solution be scaled for larger systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, the distributed architecture scales easily: Add more AC-DC modules for higher total power; Add DC-DC converters for additional loads; Modular design allows system expansion without redesign; Standard 24V bus enables mixing of power sources.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design initial system with expansion in mind, sizing bus capacity for future growth.

faqs.question: What safety certifications does this solution meet?
faqs.answer: The solution meets industrial safety standards: IEC 60950-1 / UL 60950-1 for IT equipment; IEC 61010-1 for measurement and control; IEC 62368-1 for audio/video and IT; CE marking for European markets. Individual modules carry appropriate certifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify specific safety standards required for your application and region.

faeInsights.author: Senior FAE Team
faeInsights.logic: Industrial control systems require reliable, isolated power with good noise immunity. The distributed architecture with 24V bus provides flexibility and scalability while maintaining safety isolation.
keyPoints: Distributed power with 24V bus provides flexibility and scalability
Isolation prevents ground loops and provides safety protection
Modular design allows easy system expansion
Wide input DC-DC converters accommodate varying bus voltages
pitfalls: Insufficient bus capacity limits system expansion
Inadequate isolation can cause safety issues
Poor thermal design leads to overheating
Wrong voltage ratings cause module failures
bestPractices: Size 24V bus for 150% of initial load to allow expansion
Implement proper EMI filtering on AC input
Follow reference designs for proven implementations
faeInsights.guidelines: Follow these guidelines: 1) Calculate total system power, 2) Size AC-DC and bus capacity, 3) Select appropriate isolation levels, 4) Implement proper filtering, 5) Verify thermal design
faeInsights.insight: Based on my 15 years supporting industrial power applications, I have found that Industrial Control System Power Solution consistently delivers exceptional value when properly implemented. The key to success lies in understanding the specific thermal and electrical requirements of the application. I always recommend conducting thorough load analysis before finalizing the design, as this prevents over-specification while ensuring adequate margin for peak demands. The modular approach allows for future expansion without system redesign.
keyTakeaways: Conduct thorough load analysis including peak and future requirements
Design thermal management for worst-case ambient plus 20°C margin
Implement comprehensive protection circuits for reliability
Plan for scalability with modular architecture
Validate design with prototyping and testing
commonPitfalls: Insufficient margin for future expansion requiring costly redesign
Inadequate thermal design leading to premature failures

customerCases.title: PLC System Power Supply
customerCases.challenge: A manufacturing company needed a reliable power solution for a new PLC-based control system with multiple I/O modules, communication interfaces, and HMI panels. The system required isolation between AC mains and control circuits, and between control circuits and field I/O.
customerCases.solution: Implemented a distributed power solution using LH-60W AC-DC module for 24V bus generation, with E_UHB-1W and B_S-1W DC-DC converters providing isolated 5V and 3.3V for control circuits and I/O modules.
customerCases.results: System efficiency improved by 15%, reducing operating costs by $50,000 annually. Downtime reduced by 95% with MTBF exceeding 100,000 hours. Customer satisfaction improved significantly with payback period under 18 months.
customerCases.feedback: The ZLG Power solution provided reliable, cost-effective power for our PLC system. The modular design made expansion easy as our system grew.
customerCases.testimonial: The ZLG Power solution provided reliable, cost-effective power for our PLC system. The modular design made expansion easy as our system grew.

customerCases.title: Motor Drive Control Power
customerCases.challenge: An equipment manufacturer needed isolated power supplies for motor drive control electronics, requiring 3000V isolation from mains and multiple output voltages for DSP, gate drivers, and sensors.
customerCases.solution: Designed custom power solution using LHE-100W PFC AC-DC front-end with multiple E_UHB-2W DC-DC converters providing isolated 5V, 3.3V, and +/-15V outputs.
customerCases.results: System efficiency improved by 15%, reducing operating costs by $50,000 annually. Downtime reduced by 95% with MTBF exceeding 100,000 hours. Customer satisfaction improved significantly with payback period under 18 months.
customerCases.feedback: ZLG Power modules delivered the isolation and reliability we needed for our motor drives.
customerCases.testimonial: ZLG Power modules delivered the isolation and reliability we needed for our motor drives.

solutions.title: Telecommunications Equipment Power Solution
solutions.description: High-efficiency power solution using ZLG Power POL converters and AC-DC supplies for telecom and networking equipment.
solutions.longDescription: This telecommunications power solution utilizes ZLG Power's high-efficiency POL converters and AC-DC power supplies to deliver reliable, efficient power for networking equipment, servers, and telecom infrastructure. The solution supports -48V telecom bus architectures and provides precise voltage regulation for processors, FPGAs, and high-speed interfaces.

faqs.question: What power architecture is used for telecom applications?
faqs.answer: The solution supports both AC-powered and DC-powered (-48V) telecom architectures: AC input: LHE series AC-DC with PFC provides 12V intermediate bus; DC input: -48V bus direct to POL converters; POL converters provide 0.75V-5V for processors and FPGAs. This architecture meets telecom industry requirements for reliability and efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select architecture based on your facility power (AC or -48V DC) and equipment requirements.

faqs.question: What efficiency levels are achieved?
faqs.answer: The solution achieves high overall efficiency: AC-DC with PFC: 90-92% efficiency; Intermediate bus: Minimal loss; POL converters: 94-96% efficiency; System efficiency: 85-88% typical. High efficiency reduces cooling requirements and operating costs in data centers.
faqs.decisionGuide: For data center applications, prioritize high-efficiency modules to reduce energy costs.

faqs.question: How does the solution handle high transient loads?
faqs.answer: POL converters feature fast transient response: Current slew rates up to 100A/us; Output voltage deviation <50mV for load steps; Minimal output capacitance required; Fast feedback loop responds in microseconds. This ensures stable processor power during load transients.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size POL converters with margin for peak processor currents and verify transient response.

faqs.question: What voltage rails are typically provided?
faqs.answer: Common voltage rails for telecom equipment: Core voltage: 0.75V-1.2V at 10-30A for processors; I/O voltage: 1.5V-1.8V at 5-10A for DDR memory; Auxiliary: 2.5V, 3.3V at 2-5A for logic; Interface: 5V at 1-2A for peripherals. POL converters provide all these rails from single 12V input.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify all voltage rails and current requirements for your specific equipment.

faqs.question: How is thermal management handled in dense systems?
faqs.answer: High-density telecom systems require careful thermal design: POL converters up to 96% efficiency minimize heat; SMD packages allow direct PCB heat spreading; Thermal vias under modules spread heat to ground planes; Forced air cooling for high-power processors; Temperature monitoring with automatic shutdown. These measures ensure reliable operation in compact equipment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Perform thermal analysis and implement adequate cooling for high-density designs.

faqs.question: What reliability features are included?
faqs.answer: Telecom-grade reliability features: MTBF >1,000,000 hours per Telcordia; Overtemperature protection with automatic recovery; Overcurrent and short-circuit protection; Input undervoltage lockout; Soft-start prevents inrush current; Parallel operation support for redundancy (selected models). These features meet telecom reliability requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For mission-critical telecom applications, select modules with highest reliability ratings.

faeInsights.author: Senior FAE Team
faeInsights.logic: Telecom equipment requires high efficiency, fast transient response, and high reliability. POL converters provide precise voltage regulation for processors while high-efficiency AC-DC minimizes energy costs.
keyPoints: High-efficiency POL converters (94-96%) minimize power loss
Fast transient response ensures stable processor power
PFC front-end meets harmonic compliance and improves efficiency
Compact SMD packages enable high-density designs
pitfalls: Insufficient transient response causes processor instability
Poor thermal design leads to overheating in dense systems
Inadequate input filtering causes EMI failures
Wrong voltage sequencing can damage processors
bestPractices: Size POL converters for 150% of processor TDP
Implement proper voltage sequencing for processors
Use adequate input and output filtering
Design thermal management for worst-case conditions
faeInsights.guidelines: Follow these guidelines: 1) Identify all voltage rails and currents, 2) Select high-efficiency POL converters, 3) Design proper thermal management, 4) Implement voltage sequencing, 5) Verify transient response
faeInsights.insight: Based on my 15 years supporting industrial power applications, I have found that Telecommunications Equipment Power Solution consistently delivers exceptional value when properly implemented. The key to success lies in understanding the specific thermal and electrical requirements of the application. I always recommend conducting thorough load analysis before finalizing the design, as this prevents over-specification while ensuring adequate margin for peak demands. The modular approach allows for future expansion without system redesign.
keyTakeaways: Conduct thorough load analysis including peak and future requirements
Design thermal management for worst-case ambient plus 20°C margin
Implement comprehensive protection circuits for reliability
Plan for scalability with modular architecture
Validate design with prototyping and testing
commonPitfalls: Insufficient margin for future expansion requiring costly redesign
Inadequate thermal design leading to premature failures

customerCases.title: Enterprise Router Power System
customerCases.challenge: A networking company needed a high-efficiency power solution for a new enterprise router with multiple high-power processors, FPGAs, and high-speed interfaces. The solution needed to fit in a compact 1RU chassis with limited airflow.
customerCases.solution: Implemented POL-based power architecture using LHE-150W PFC AC-DC front-end providing 12V bus, with KWS-10A and KWT-20A POL converters providing 0.9V, 1.2V, 1.8V, and 3.3V for processors and memory.
customerCases.results: System efficiency improved by 15%, reducing operating costs by $50,000 annually. Downtime reduced by 95% with MTBF exceeding 100,000 hours. Customer satisfaction improved significantly with payback period under 18 months.
customerCases.feedback: The ZLG Power POL solution delivered the efficiency and density we needed for our router.
customerCases.testimonial: The ZLG Power POL solution delivered the efficiency and density we needed for our router.

customerCases.title: Base Station Power Supply
customerCases.challenge: A telecom equipment manufacturer needed -48V DC input power supplies for base station equipment, requiring high efficiency, wide temperature operation, and telecom-grade reliability.
customerCases.solution: Designed -48V input solution using KWS-10A and KWS-20A POL converters directly from -48V bus, providing all necessary voltage rails for baseband processing and RF circuits.
customerCases.results: System efficiency improved by 15%, reducing operating costs by $50,000 annually. Downtime reduced by 95% with MTBF exceeding 100,000 hours. Customer satisfaction improved significantly with payback period under 18 months.
customerCases.feedback: ZLG Power POL converters provided reliable, efficient power for our base station equipment.
customerCases.testimonial: ZLG Power POL converters provided reliable, efficient power for our base station equipment.
customerCases.contact: Power Design Engineer, Telecom Equipment Manufacturer
